title: Module 2 quiz: Targeting in the Advantage+ era

questions.prompt: Sweet-spot audience size for broad prospecting in a mature account?

choices.text: 2-10M. Below 1M the algorithm can't find enough lookalike events to stabilize EAR predictions; above 50M, broad only works if creative pre-qualifies. The 2-10M range gives ML enough room to explore while staying tight enough that creative + pixel signal keep delivery focused.

questions.explanation: 2-10M is the comfortable range; smaller is unstable, larger needs strong creative.

questions.prompt: Building a lookalike. Three seed options. Which is best?

choices.text: 5k CRM-uploaded purchasers from last 180 days with name + email + phone. Recent, large, high-quality buyer signal — uncorrupted by tracking loss because the data came from your business, not the pixel. Site-visitor seeds point at window-shoppers; old seeds reflect past buying behavior, not current. Value-based version with LTV column weights toward top customers.

questions.explanation: Recent CRM purchasers = clean buyer signal. Size and quality both matter.

choices.text: 5-15 ads mixing short-form video (vertical 9:16, 15-30s), static images/carousels, and UGC style. ASC rotates aggressively across users and placements; thin libraries underutilize the engine. Within days a few creatives dominate delivery — refresh weekly: add 2-3 new, remove bottom 2-3, don't pause winners.

questions.explanation: 5-15 mixed-format ads, weekly rotation; don't pause winners.

questions.prompt: Three prospecting ad sets (broad + 1% LAL + interest stack) in one campaign. Why is performance unstable?

choices.text: Audience overlap. The three sets share most users, so they bid against each other in the auction — Meta picks one ad per user but the overlap creates inefficiency and fragments learning across sets that should be one. Audit with the audience overlap tool; >20% overlap = consolidate. Modern practice: one acquisition audience (broad OR a single lookalike) plus separated retargeting.

Refresh CRM customer-list upload (so it includes recent purchasers), audit audience overlap between active ad sets, confirm post-purchase exclusion is still wired, check retargeting frequency for fatigue (>10), verify geo/age/language settings, refresh lookalike seeds. 15-minute monthly habit. Most 'account stopped working' stories trace to skipped hygiene, not algorithm changes.

questions.explanation: Monthly hygiene prevents most performance decay.
